schema.title: Response Health Db Health Db Get

get.description:
Identity the iOS app pings to verify a URL is really a SplitBack backend before adopting it
(the join-link confirm screen). Reveals nothing sensitive.

post.description:
Guest login for the DEMO backend only: mints an ephemeral user (a name, no OAuth/email) and seeds it
a populated, isolated sample app. 404 elsewhere so prod/dev never expose it.

Begin connecting Splitwise to the *signed-in* user. The OAuth state is bound to the verified caller
server-side (no client-supplied identifier), so the resulting token can only attach to the caller.

get.description:
Anonymous *sign in with Splitwise* (first-time). The token binds to the user resolved from the Splitwise
identity at callback; `invite` enrolls a new user on a claimed server. No caller-supplied identifier.
Splitwise groups the caller is a member of that were deleted through the app (restorable), newest first.

schema.title: Response List Deleted Groups Groups Deleted Get

post.description:
Restore a deleted Splitwise group (undelete on Splitwise + its expenses, clear the flag, re-sync). Any
member of the group can call this.

schema.title: Response List Members Groups  Group Id  Members Get

delete.description:
Permanently delete the expense (hard delete + receipt cleanup). For a Splitwise-linked expense in an
active group it also deletes it on Splitwise (so balances stay in parity); `propagate=false` keeps the
Splitwise copy. To exclude an expense from your budget without deleting it, use the per-user override.

put.description:
Link (or unlink, with null) this expense to a bank/manual transaction. A local-only field assigned
directly — a separate endpoint from PATCH so it never triggers a Splitwise push or touches splits, and
so null can clear the link (PATCH's transaction_id can only set).

patch.description:
Set the caller's per-user budget overrides (include in spending / cash flow) in `expense_overrides`,
keyed by owner + expense. Only provided fields change (exclude_unset); null clears that field, and the row
is dropped once every field is null. Never propagates to Splitwise and never touches balances.

schema.title: Response List Receipts Expenses  Expense Id  Receipts Get
Response List Items Plaid Items Get

patch.description:
Set the caller's per-user account overrides (display name, kind, Goals-analytics inclusion flags) in
`account_overrides`, keyed by owner + account. Plaid sync only touches name/type/balance/currency/mask, so
these survive a re-sync.

Set (or clear, with null) the caller's per-user category override in `transaction_overrides`, keyed by
owner + transaction. The row also carries budget-inclusion flags (set via /override) and is dropped only
once every field is null. Plaid sync only touches description/amount/currency/date/category/pending, so
this survives a re-sync.

Set the caller's per-user budget overrides (include in spending / cash flow) on the transaction,
keyed by owner + transaction. Only provided fields change (exclude_unset); coexists with the category
override and never touches balances.

put.description:
Replace a transaction's line items (upsert by id, drop-orphan). A separate endpoint from PATCH so
it never touches the category-override (whose omitted-clears semantics we must not trip).

delete.description: Delete the caller's own account and personal data (App Store account-deletion requirement).

get.description:
The caller's current balance with each person, mirroring Splitwise's Friends tab. Sourced from
Splitwise's authoritative `getFriends()` when a token is connected; otherwise computed server-side from the
caller's group expenses (self-hosted fallback). Positive net = that person owes the caller.

schema.title: Response Group Balances Groups  Group Id  Balances Get

get.description:
Proxy a Splitwise expense's receipt image. Splitwise serves receipts from an authenticated API
endpoint, so we fetch with the stored OAuth token and stream the bytes back to the app. `size`
(e.g. `original`) requests a higher resolution for the full-screen view.

post.description: Cold backfill (full window). Stamps the incremental cursor so /sync/expenses takes over.
Pull-to-refresh the Groups list: refresh group metadata + members.
Pull-to-refresh the People list: refresh the users directory (members + current user).

Pull-to-refresh expenses: incremental pull since the stored cursor (or `since` override).
Catches edits/settle-ups and hard-deletes expenses Splitwise has deleted.
Drill-in: refresh one Splitwise group's metadata + members and its expenses only.
Drill-in: refresh the expenses shared with one friend (resolved to their Splitwise user id).
Drill-in: refresh a single expense (upsert, or delete when Splitwise has deleted it).

Pull-to-refresh the Friends list: cache the token owner's Splitwise friends (identity), so a
friend with no shared group still resolves to a name/avatar.

Pull the token owner's recent Splitwise notifications into the generic notifications store,
pruned to the `notifications_retention_count` server setting.

get.description: The caller's cached notifications (any source), newest first.

schema.title: Response List Notifications Splitwise Notifications Get

post.description:
Clone a Splitwise-linked group into a new self-hosted group (native, full-featured copies of
expenses incl. provenance, splits, items, and receipts), then mark the source superseded so balances don't
double-count. With SPLITWISE_RECEIPT_DOWNLOAD_ENABLED, each Splitwise receipt's original image is
downloaded into MinIO so the local copy is self-contained.

Standalone flow: download original Splitwise receipt images for a group's expenses into MinIO as
native Receipt rows. Gated by SPLITWISE_RECEIPT_DOWNLOAD_ENABLED; skips expenses that already have
a receipt (idempotent).

Kick off a one-time, best-effort download of every not-yet-downloaded Splitwise receipt across the
caller's groups (newest-first, rate-limited). Runs in the BACKGROUND so the caller can navigate away.
Gated by SPLITWISE_RECEIPT_BACKFILL_ENABLED; idempotent (a re-tap just skips ones already present).

get.description: The caller's partner connections (incoming/outgoing pending + accepted), each with the other party.

post.description: Invite a partner to connect (pending until they accept).
Accept a pending invite (only the invited party). Idempotent on an already-accepted one.

delete.description: Decline a pending invite or disconnect an accepted partner (either party).

post.description: Registers (idempotently) the caller's APNs device token for push.

delete.description: Removes the caller's device token (sign-out / push disabled).

get.description: The caller's notifications across all sources (Splitwise + app), newest first.
The caller's notification preference tokens (push/feed mutes). Empty = everything pushed + shown.

put.description: Replace the caller's full set of preference tokens (delete-all + insert).

get.description:
Every preference blob belonging to the caller (empty in open mode). Each is an opaque JSON string
the client decodes itself — e.g. the per-user category config under key `categories.v1`.

put.description:
Store (create or replace) the caller's preference blob under `key`. Scoped to `owner_identifier`,
so users sharing a backend keep independent preferences.

get.description:
Search the OFX-importable institution directory (Intuit FIDIR Web Connect banks). Reference data —
no user scope.

InstitutionResponse.title: InstitutionResponse
InstitutionResponse.description:
A bank/credit institution that supports OFX file (Web Connect) export — for the in-app
"banks you can import from" directory. Sourced from Intuit's FIDIR list.

NotificationPrefs.title: NotificationPrefs
NotificationPrefs.description:
The caller's notification preference tokens (`"<channel>:<selector>"`). Used for both the GET
response and the PUT body (replace-set semantics).

ReceiptDownloadStarted.title: ReceiptDownloadStarted
ReceiptDownloadStarted.description:
The bulk download-all kickoff: the work runs in the background (the caller can leave). `pending` is how
many receipts were queued; `enabled` is false (nothing started) when the backfill setting is off.

ServerSettingsResponse.title: ServerSettingsResponse
ServerSettingsResponse.description: The full server-settings registry resolved to current values.

ServerSettingsUpdate.title: ServerSettingsUpdate
ServerSettingsUpdate.description: Any subset of the registry; only provided keys change (PATCH semantics).
